description:
Drop the underscores on `_ft` / `_return_descr` in build_fn_signature. Read `ft.vars[entry.params[i]]` per native param; emit raw f64 for float-only Descrs, raw i64 for int-only Descrs, tagged i64 otherwise. Same for the return based on `return_descr`. Delete the deferral comment at ir_codegen.rs:587-592.

Unit test on `build_fn_signature` alone (no codegen): construct a tiny module with `fn f(n: int) = n + 1` proven monomorphic; assert the produced Cranelift signature has `(raw_i64, i64) -> raw_i64` (or in Cranelift terms, AbiParam types I64 with no special markers but driven by the Descr path). Float case asserts AbiParam(F64) for the first param.

This step is a no-op for codegen — only the sig changes. STEP.2 wires the entry block to match.
title: VR.4 follow-up STEP.1: Descr-driven build_fn_signature
